column1: # Enforcement-class proof manifest — single source of truth binding each
# enforcement class to (a) its capability label, (b) the BPF-LSM hook(s) that
# implement it, and (c) the whitepaper claim phrase that must appear in
# docs/ENFORCEMENT_SEMANTICS_WHITEPAPER.md under the matching label section.
# CI, that this manifest, the harness probes in enforcement_proof.sh, and the
# whitepaper labels are mutually consistent. The behavioral proof that each
# class actually returns -EPERM is the harness itself, run on the kernel matrix.
# A class may NOT be labeled ENFORCED unless it has both a harness probe here and
# a claim in the whitepaper. This makes "every ENFORCED claim points to a green
# per-class test" structural, not editorial. See docs/ENFORCEMENT_WEDGE_STRATEGY.md.
